result.markdown: --- title: "[TestResult]{.doc-object-name .doc-class .doc-label .doc-label-dataclass}" body-classes: doc-api-page page-navigation: false html-table-processing: none --- ::: {.doc-subject} The outcome of a log-rank group comparison test. ::: ::: {.doc-usage-source} Usage [Source](https://github.com/rich-iannone/greenwood/blob/main/greenwood/_tests.py#L30-L126){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} ::: ::: {.doc-signature .doc-Kind.CLASS} ```python TestResult(statistic, df, p_value, method, observed=dict(), expected=dict()) ``` ::: ::: {.doc-text} This class stores the results of `logrank_test` or `pairwise_logrank_test` in a structured format. Access test statistics, significance (p-value), and per-group observed vs. expected event counts. ::: ## Attributes {.doc-attributes} ::: {.doc-definition-items} [statistic]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : The chi-square test statistic. Larger values indicate stronger evidence against the null hypothesis of equal survival across groups. [df]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [int]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : Degrees of freedom for the chi-square distribution (number of groups minus one for `logrank_test`, always 1 for pairwise tests). [p_value]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : Upper-tail chi-square p-value. The probability of observing a chi-square statistic this large or larger under the null hypothesis of equal survival. Small p-values (typically p < 0.05) indicate significant differences between groups. [method]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [str]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : Human-readable description of the test method and its configuration, e.g., "Log-rank test", "Stratified log-rank test", "G-rho test (rho=1, gamma=0)". [observed]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [dict[Any, float]]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : Dictionary mapping each group label to its observed (actual) weighted event count. Useful for understanding which groups contribute more events. [expected]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [dict[Any, float]]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : Dictionary mapping each group label to its expected event count under the null hypothesis of equal survival. Comparison of observed vs. expected reveals which groups have more or fewer events than expected. ::: ## Details {.doc-details} For a significant result (p_value < 0.05), examine the `observed` and `expected` dictionaries to see which groups experienced more or fewer events than expected. Groups with observed > expected have worse (shorter) survival; groups with observed < expected have better (longer) survival. ## Examples {.doc-examples} Run a log-rank test and examine results: ::: {#8808c8f2 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import greenwood as gw # Load data and build a right-censored response lung = gw.load_dataset("lung", backend="polars") y = gw.Surv.right(lung["time"], event=(lung["status"] == 2)) # Run a log-rank test comparing survival by sex result = gw.logrank_test(y, group=lung["sex"]) result ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ``` TestResult(method='Log-rank test', statistic=10.3267, df=1, p_value=0.001311) ``` ::: ::: Access individual components. The chi-square statistic: ::: {#a61602bb .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} result.statistic ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ``` 10.326741954885637 ``` ::: ::: The p-value for significance: ::: {#b208787e .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} result.p_value ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ``` 0.0013111645203554869 ``` ::: ::: Observed event counts per group (actual events in data): ::: {#776de8a5 .cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} result.observed ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=4} ``` {1: 112.0, 2: 53.0} ``` ::: ::: Expected event counts per group (under null hypothesis): ::: {#b38ef21d .cell execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code} result.expected ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=5} ``` {1: 91.58173902957279, 2: 73.41826097042721} ``` ::: ::: Test description: ::: {#a80f667f .cell execution_count=6} ``` {.python .cell-code} result.method ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=6} ``` 'Log-rank test' ``` ::: :::
